Off Calendar Jun-19-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Shahrad Milanfar, esq. (510-658-3600) for Defendant.
No other appearances.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT LEDCOR CONSTRUCTION, INC.'s Motion For Leave To Amend Answer:

Defendant and Cross-Complainant Ledcor Construction Inc.'s motion for leave to amend answer is granted. "Leave to amend a pleading, including an answer, is entrusted to the sound discretion of the trial court." (Hong Sang Market, Inc. v. Peng (2018) 20 Cal.App.5th 474, 488 [no abuse of discretion in allowing amendment to add res judicata defense].) Plaintiff has not shown that Ledcor unduly delayed in seeking leave to amend, or that he would be prejudiced if the amendment is allowed. (See id. [plaintiff failed to show prejudice in defendant's delay in seeking leave to amend, where issue was purely legal in nature and it was unclear what discovery plaintiff might have conducted that would bear upon the new defense].)

Order is signed in open court.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-26-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Andrew Johnson, Esq. 510-444-5020 of Bennett & Johnson LLp for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Alexander Catalona, Esq. 510-658-3600 of Becherer Kannett & Schweitzer for Defendant Ledcorp.
Robert Lawton, Esq. 415-986-5900 of Gordon Rees Scully Masukhani LLP for Defendant Bratton Masonry, Inc. through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT/CROSS COMPLAINANT LEDCOR CONSTRUCTION, INC.'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Cross-Complaint.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Cross-Complainant Ledcor Construction, Inc.'s motion for leave to file first amended cross-complaint is GRANTED. California has a liberal policy of allowing leave to amend and where undue prejudice to opposing parties can be avoided, denial of leave to amend that prevents a meritorious cause of action constitutes an abuse of discretion. (Redevelopment Agency v. Herrold (1978) 86 Cal.App.3d 1024, 1031.) Ledcor's did not unreasonably delay in bringing this motion as it had no reason to sue ACCO prior to the September 28, 2018 announcement that ACCO's insurer would no longer indemnify Ledcor. Moreover, evidence putting Ledcor on notice of ACCO's alleged liability was not discovered until the October 17, 2018 deposition of ACCO employee Michael Castillo. Taking Ledcor's representations that no further discovery will be necessary given the contractual nature of its claims against ACCO as true, there is no prejudice to other parties. Therefore, leave to amend is provided, but discovery will not be reopened.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)
